Responsibilities:

This position is responsible for assisting in the repair and overhaul of equipment, refrigeration, heating, and ventilation equipment to ensure customer needs are met efficiently, accurately, and on time.* Provides service to the customer base by repairing and overhauling equipment, such as compressors, water pumps, air handlers, and electrical and mechanical control devices in a safe, efficient, and timely manner that exceeds customers’ expectations
* Provides general repair work on refrigeration, heating, and ventilation equipment, such as air conditioning units, evaporative condensers, ice machine equipment, water pumps, gas furnaces, compressors, and electric motors in a safe and timely manner.

* Drives company vehicle in a safe manner.
* Performs other related duties and projects as assigned by management.


Qualifications:
* High school diploma/GED.
* EPA certification.
* Certification from an accredited vocational\\-technical or trade school is a plus!
* Minimum 5\\+ years of commercial refrigeration experience required.
* Experience working on refrigeration equipment in convenience stores or supermarkets is a strong plus.
* Familiarity with single systems and rack systems along with previous work on display cases and walk\\-in boxes is very helpful.
* Must have a good driving record and be able to work independently.
* Emergency on\\-call availability is a basic requirement.
* Must be able to lift to 60 lbs. with no assistance and also be able to sit and/or stand for several hours at a time.

title: Maintenance Technician
description:
Overview:
**Lorien Mount Airy** is seeking a **Maintenance Technician** to join our dedicated and supportive team . This role is essential in maintaining a safe, clean, and well‑functioning environment that supports the care and comfort of our patients and staff .
The **Maintenance Technician** is responsible for the proper and safe preparation, repair, application, and ongoing maintenance of painted and related surfaces—both **interior and exterior**—as well as general facility upkeep.### **Schedule \\& Pay**

* ✅ **Full\\-Time**
* **$18\\.00 – $20\\.00 per hour**

### **Key Responsibilities**


* Prepare, repair, paint, and maintain interior and exterior surfaces
* Perform routine facility maintenance and minor repairs
* + ️ Ensure work is completed safely and in compliance with standards
* Support a clean, functional, and safe care environment
* Collaborate with the maintenance team to meet facility needs

### **Full\\-Time Benefits**


* **PTO**
	+ 80 hours Vacation
	+ 40 hours Sick Time (roll‑over eligible)
* Personal Days
* **7 Paid Major Holidays**
	+ - ️ *Double time paid when worked*
* CareFirst Medical Insurance
* Tuition Assistance
* HRA Fund
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
* Flexible Spending Account (FSA)

### **Employee Perks**


* ️ Employee Discounts
* + Earn Points, Earn Rewards Program
* Professional Development Opportunities
* Attendance Rewards
* Employee Referral Program
* **Same‑Day Pay available for****all employees!**
  


Responsibilities:
### **✅** **Qualifications \\& Responsibilities**


* Complete work orders and repair requests in a timely and efficient manner
* ️ Perform routine **preventive maintenance** inside and outside of the building
* Oversee and coordinate work performed by **outside contractors** as needed
* **Carpentry experience preferred**
* ️ Previous experience in **maintenance or a related field**
* Familiarity with common **maintenance tools and equipment**

❄️* **Strong HVAC knowledge or certification required**
* Ability to safely handle a **physical workload**
* Experience with **plumbing, painting, and basic electrical work**
  


Qualifications:
* Full Time
* Schedule: 8:30a\\-5p
* On\\-Call weekend rotation \\- required

  

At **Lorien Mount Airy**, we’re more than just a healthcare facility, we’re a tight\\-knit community that genuinely cares for our residents and our team members. Our mission is to help every employee build a meaningful, successful career, and we’re proud to have staff who’ve been with us for 20 years or more. When you join us, you’re not just taking a job — you’re joining a family that supports your growth every step of the way.  

Conveniently located right off Interstate 70 in Carroll County, MD, our campus is easily accessible from Frederick, Howard, and Montgomery Counties — all within a short commute. (Fun fact: The Town of Mount Airy spans all four counties!) **Our facility offers a full continuum of care, including:*** **Assisted Living**
* **Long\\-Term Care**

* **Ventilator Care** **with on\\-site** **respiratory therapists**

Whether you're just starting out or looking to advance in your healthcare career, **Lorien Mount Airy** **o**ffers the support, stability, and opportunities to help you thrive.
Award\\-Winning Care:  

Proudly recognized as **“Best Assisted Living”** and **“Best Retirement Community”**by The Daily Record's Reader Rankings. *Lorien Health Services is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or protected Veteran status.*

title: HVAC Maintenance Technician
description:
Southern Home Services is a residential HVAC, Plumbing and Electrical company that acquires, owns, and operates businesses around the United States. We are seeking business leaders to join our growing team and become an integral part of our company's future.

**Are you ready to be Part of Something BIGGER?**

**Presidential**, a Southern Home Services Company, is looking for a dynamic\\-**HVAC Maintenance Technician** to join a thriving business that believes in work life balance and cares about YOU!

**In this role, you will be responsible for:**

* Clean, adjust and repair HVAC systems\\- air handling units, ductwork, vents, condensation drainage
* Perform routine service work on HVAC systems
* Providing a top\\-notch customer experience and effectively communicating to homeowners
* Additional duties as assigned.

**Required Qualifications:**

* Valid Driver's License with a minimum of three years of driving experience is required
* Great Attitude with a Dedication to Quality Work
* Customer Service Oriented
* Ability to deal effectively with a diversity of individuals at all organizational levels
* Adaptable, flexible, and innovative team player
* Strong Troubleshooting and Critical Thinking Skills
* EPA Certification
* 1\\+ Years of HVAC experience, preferably in the residential market

**The physical requirements of this job include:**

* Lifting 50 pounds regularly and up to 100 pounds occasionally
* Climbing ladders, accessing attics, navigating tight spaces, driving, entering crawlspaces, pushing, carrying, pulling, using hand tools, and performing fine detail work.
* Ability to drive.
* Working in various environments, including indoor and outdoor settings, attics, crawlspaces.
* Ability to stand, walk and sit; talk or hear, both in person and by telephone.
* Use hands to handle or feel objects or controls; reach with hands and arms.
* Regularly stoop, kneel, bend, and crouch.
* The work requires regular exposure to extreme temperatures both indoors and outdoors, such as heat or cold, and adverse weather conditions such as wind, rain, ice or snow.

**About this role:**


An HVAC Service Technician is responsible for the service, maintenance, and repair of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems in residential and commercial settings. This role involves diagnosing system issues, performing routine and emergency repairs, and ensuring systems operate efficiently and safely. Technicians interact with customers to explain problems and solutions, adhere to safety regulations, and maintain a clean and organized work environment. They may also be involved in system inspections, adjustments, and upgrades, while keeping detailed records of service performed. Strong technical skills, customer service, and adherence to industry standards are crucial for success in this role.

**In this role, you will be responsible for:**

* Clean, adjust, and repair HVAC systems, including air handling units, ductwork, vents, and condensation drainage.
* Diagnose and perform service work on HVAC systems in residential settings to ensure optimal performance.
* Provide expert HVAC solutions to customers, addressing their needs and offering professional recommendations.
* Additional duties as assigned.

**Required Qualifications:**

* 3\\-5\\+ years of HVAC experience, preferably with a focus on residential markets

* Valid Driver’s License with a minimum of three years of driving experience is required
* Possesses strong troubleshooting and critical thinking skills for resolving complex HVAC issues.
* Demonstrates a positive attitude and a strong commitment to high\\-quality workmanship.
* Prioritizes exceptional customer service and effectively addresses diverse client needs.
* Skilled in interacting professionally with individuals at all organizational levels.
* Flexible and innovative team player, thriving in dynamic environments and adapting to new challenges.

**The physical requirements of this job include:**

  

* Lifting 50 pounds regularly and up to 100 pounds occasionally
* Climbing ladders, accessing attics, navigating tight spaces, driving, entering crawlspaces, pushing, carrying, pulling, using hand tools, and performing fine detail work.
* Ability to drive.
* Working in various environments, including indoor and outdoor settings, attics, crawlspaces.
* Ability to stand, walk and sit; talk or hear, both in person and by telephone.
* Use hands to handle or feel objects or controls; reach with hands and arms.
* Regularly stoop, kneel, bend, and crouch.
* The work requires regular exposure to extreme temperatures both indoors and outdoors, such as heat or cold, and adverse weather conditions such as wind, rain, ice or snow.

At Ingerman, the Maintenance Technician is responsible for performing repairs, troubleshooting, and carrying out preventative maintenance on assigned property/properties.  **Key Job Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To:**

* Performs repairs, troubleshoots mechanical systems, and completes preventative maintenance

* Minimum of two years of maintenance or construction experience

* HVAC certification preferred
* Must be able to work a flex schedule and be on\\-call for emergencies on weekends, evenings, and holidays

**PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES**

**About us:**  

We are a relatively small but fast\\-growing organization. We value our employees and are seek new ways to improve the employee experience and benefits. We believe in allowing our people to grow their careers along with us. *Are you ready to join?*

title: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Technician (HVAC)
description:
If you’re an experienced HVAC installation and Service Technician and one of your favorite parts is seeing a satisfied customer, we have an immediate opening for an HVAC Technician.

This is a customer\\-facing, residential service role where you’ll meet the customer in their home, complete the necessary repairs, maintenance or installation, and talk with them about recommended solutions. You will succeed here by providing a world\\-class experience and caring about the customer's needs.

This installation role includes mostly residential and some light commercial work. Help deliver customer’s heating and cooling needs by providing quality system installations including Split Systems, Duct Work, Air Handlers, Furnaces, Condensers, thermostats, IAQ products, smart home devices and more. In this role you will learn valuable trade skills and you will succeed here by providing a world\\-class experience and caring about the customer's needs.

At Aire Serv, we’re known for our excellent customer service, professional image, and Done Right Promise. We’re looking for someone who can live up to our high standards of service and professionalism. If that’s you, apply today, and someone from our team will respond within 24 hours.

**Why You’ll Want to Join Our Team**

Here are just a few of the benefits you’ll receive when joining us at Aire Serv as an HVAC Technician:

· Paid training at our company facility, manufacturer’s facilities, and corporate office – we cover travel, meals, lodging \\& all training.

· Branded company van

· iPad loaded with all the apps and forms you’ll need

· Uniforms provided

· Paid time off the first year with increases each year, plus paid holidays

· Year\\-round employment

· Additional earning potential with spiffs, commission, and bonuses for performance

· Career path based on your goals and aspirations

**Your Responsibilities as an HVAC Service Technician**

As an HVAC Service Technician, you are vital in taking care of our clients by providing them with the highest\\-quality service and maintenance of their home’s HVAC systems.

· **Deliver World\\-Class Service** – Deliver an amazing experience for our customers.

· **Troubleshoot and Provide System Maintenance** – Consult with clients regarding their heating and air conditioning systems, understand their concerns, and diagnose and fix the issue correctly.

· **Deliver World\\-Class Quality Installations** – Deliver an amazing system and install experience to our customers.

· **Advise on System Upgrades, Improvements, and Service Plans** – Help customers uncover additional opportunities and needs related to their home comfort and air quality while offering your expert recommendation for the best solution.

**What We Want from You**

· Culture alignment

· Rockstars only

· Bought into the vision

· Meets our expectations

**To Succeed in this Role**

We employ a front\\-line service system that, when followed, will consistently generate 5\\-star reviews.

The office team has your back and will ensure you have sufficient time with each customer to do a thorough and complete job.

You are willing to take on the hot attics and crawl spaces to get the job done. And you are willing to take the time to explain things to the customer in a way they will understand.

You are committed to continued training, taking advantage of company\\-provided opportunities, and investing time into your personal and professional development.

**How We’ll Measure Performance**

Here are some ways we’ll measure your performance in the role.

· **Punctuality, Reliability, and Availability** – These things impact our customers and the rest of the team.

· **Quality of Work** – The work must be done correctly the first time.

· **Efficiency** – Doing the job well and promptly are critical to success in this role.

· **Customer Satisfaction** – You will be responsible for delivering an amazing customer experience and ensuring high customer satisfaction.

· **Key Service Metrics** – We use a scorecard system so that you have clear goals that you are confident you can achieve and will know exactly how you are performing.

**How to Know If This is the Job for You**

· **You are team oriented.** When you join Aire Serv as an HVAC technician, you will be part of our local team and a larger community of like\\-minded service technicians.

· **You love a smooth process**. Our HVAC techs follow our Aire Serv process, ensuring a consistent and amazing experience for our clients, while growing their income and career.

· **You empathize with the customer**. You understand what a customer is going through and show that you care by listening to their concerns. You walk them through their options in a way that the customer understands. You are there to help today and in the future.

**Job Requirements**

These are the minimum requirements to be considered for this position.

· Experience in residential HVAC

· Valid driver's license and clean driving record

· Ability to lift 60 lbs.

· Excellent communication skills with customers and office team

· Professional appearance and personality

**Benefits \\& Pay**

The pay range for this position depends on your experience and knowledge as an HVAC service technician.

We’re looking to hire only the best. So, if that’s you, apply today. If you meet the requirements for the position, our system will schedule you for an interview immediately.

Pay: $30\\.00 \\- $38\\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

description:
**Pay:** $25 \\- $35 per hour plus commission**Sign On Bonus:** Up to $5,000 based on experience, license, and interview**Earning potential over $100K/year based on performance****Full\\-time, year\\-round work**  

Join ARS\\-Rescue Rooter, the nation’s largest provider of residential **HVAC, heating, air conditioning, plumbing, and electrical services**, with more than **7,000 team members and over 45 years of experience** delivering trusted home comfort solutions.  

We are hiring skilled **HVAC Service Technicians** to diagnose, troubleshoot, service, and repair residential **heating and air conditioning systems**. This role supports homeowners by providing reliable **HVAC repair, maintenance, and system diagnostics**.### **What We Offer**

* Insurance available after 31 days
* Low\\-cost medical (as low as $5/week)
* Dental, vision, HSA/FSA
* 401(k) with company match
* 13 days PTO \\+ 8 paid holidays
* Company\\-paid life insurance
* Take\\-home service vehicle \\+ gas card
* Uniforms \\+ cleaning service
* Weekly direct deposit

  

As a **Residential HVAC Service Technician**, you will:* Troubleshoot and diagnose residential **HVAC systems**, including furnaces, heat pumps, and air conditioning units
* Perform **HVAC repairs, service calls, and preventative maintenance**
* Identify system issues and recommend appropriate **heating and air conditioning solutions**
* Ensure all **HVAC service work** meets company standards and local code requirements
* Communicate clearly with homeowners about system performance and recommended repairs
* Deliver professional customer service while completing **HVAC service and repair work**


This position offers consistent work, strong earning potential, and the opportunity to grow your career in the **residential HVAC industry**. **What You Need:*** 2\\-3 years of **residential HVAC experience**
* EPA certification (or ability to obtain)
* Valid driver’s license with clean driving record
* Must pass background check and drug screening
* Ability to work in attics, crawlspaces, and lift heavy HVAC equipment


For questions about the role, you may contact our recruiting team at 202\\-674\\-3909\\.

title: Maintenance Technician
description:
Gates Hudson is seeking a Maintenance Technician to support the day\\-to\\-day physical maintenance of one of our residential communities. This role is responsible for performing corrective and preventative maintenance to ensure a safe, functional, and well\\-maintained living environment for residents.  


The ideal candidate is dependable, safety\\-conscious, and skilled in a wide range of maintenance and repair tasks.  


What You'll Do  


Maintenance \\& Repairs  


Perform routine, preventative, and emergency maintenance on apartments, buildings, and common areas  


Complete service requests promptly and efficiently  


Diagnose and repair plumbing, electrical, HVAC, appliances, carpentry, drywall, flooring, and related systems  


Perform unit turnovers and prepare apartments for new residents  


Preventative Maintenance \\& Safety  


Conduct scheduled preventative maintenance and update logs  


Follow OSHA, EPA, ADA, and Fair Housing regulations  


Identify and address unsafe conditions throughout the property  


Properly store and handle tools, equipment, and hazardous materials  


Resident Service \\& Team Support  


Enter occupied apartments professionally and in accordance with policy  


Promote positive resident relationships through quality workmanship  


Assist other technicians and support work at other properties as needed  


Administrative \\& Inventory Support  


Maintain organized stock rooms, tools, and maintenance areas  


Track inventory and report shortages  


Follow key control, reporting, and documentation procedures  


Schedule \\& Requirements  


Participation in on\\-call rotation as needed  


Reliable personal transportation required  


What We're Looking For  


Required  


High school diploma or GED  


Two or more years of maintenance experience, preferably in residential property management  


Ability to perform plumbing, electrical, HVAC, carpentry, drywall, and general maintenance work  


Strong safety awareness and attention to detail  


Ability to work independently and as part of a team  


Preferred  


CFC Type II or Universal certification

title: HVAC Department Maintenance Technician
description:
The Maintenance Technician provides preventative maintenance and upkeep on school division buildings, furnishings and ground. The Maintenance Technician may perform advanced general maintenance or work primarily with one or more specialties such as carpentry, roofing, groundskeeping, plumbing, masonry, painting, electrical, HVAC on a commercial level, grounds keeping, etc.

  

This position will support the HVAC Department and will be responsbile for servicing, maintaining, and repairing HVAC systems across K\\-12 educational facilities. This role supports the daily operations of the school division and serves as essential personnel as needed.

 **Essential Job Functions:**

* Keeps the building and premises, including sidewalks, driveways, and grounds properly maintained at all times.
* Conducts routine and preventative maintenance activity relative to buildings, furnishings, equipment and fixtures.
* Performs minor building modifications such as installing office partitions, doors, drywalls, including simple wiring.
* Maintains an inventory of all chemical and general building supplies, including equipment and fuel.
* Requisitions replacements for supplies on appropriate forms far enough in advance to ensure timely delivery that will not hinder the maintenance of the building.
* Complies with the local codes and ordinances pertaining to the storage, disposal and recycling of trash, rubbish and waste.
* Assists with removing snow during inclement weather from parking areas, including unloading sand and rock salt.
* Performs miscellaneous duties such as courier service, and delivery of equipment, furniture, and supplies to school.


Click here to read the full Job Description for this role.

 **Education and Experience:**

  

Required

* High School Diploma or GED


Preferred

* Current (valid, non\\-expired) Driver’s License and Commercial Driver’s License (CDL) with a school bus endorsement
* Two (2\\) years of experience working in maintenance, ideally within a public school system or other Educational setting

**Special Requirements:**

* A Commercial Driver’s License (CDL) with a school bus endorsement may be required for select roles within the Maintenance department.
* The employee must pass a yearly physical and participate fully in the drug/alcohol screening program.

**About Spotsylvania County Public Schools:**

  

Comprised of 31 schools and home to a diverse student population of over 24,000, Spotsylvania County Public Schools is one of the largest School Divisions in the Commonwealth of Virginia.

  

So what sets us apart? Choosing SCPS means joining a highly collaborative team of professionals who inspire, challenge and support one another to be their very best everyday. As a team member, you become part of something much bigger \\- Your success directly influences our student's success. Here, YOU make a difference.

  

The financial benefits of working for Spotsylvania County Public Schools include a competitive salary, monthly PTO accrual, robust healthcare benefit plans, enrollment or transition into the Virginia Retirement System (VRS) with defined contributions and potential employer match, FMLA and Short\\-Term Disability leave, Tuition Reimbursement, Employee Assistance Programs (EAPs), and employer paid Life Insurance.

  

To learn more about Spotsylvania County Public Schools and our teams, please visit our website at https://www.spotsylvania.k12\\.va.us/.

 **Employment Terms:** Employment may be offered as a 12\\-Month (243 Day), full\\-time position. The work schedule for this position is 7\\.5 hours worked per day, excluding lunch. This position is evaluated annually.

 **Benefits Eligibility:** Eligible

  

Together, we prepare our students for their future. We look forward to welcoming you to the **\\#WeAreSpotsy** team.

title: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Installer Technician
description:
**Job Summary**  
Join our dynamic team as a Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Installer Technician! In this energized role, you will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and repairing HVAC systems to ensure optimal indoor air quality and comfort. Your mechanical expertise and field service experience will drive your success as you work on a variety of projects, from residential to commercial settings. This position offers a fantastic opportunity to develop your skills in HVAC design, refrigeration, plumbing, and equipment repair while contributing to the comfort and safety of our clients’ environments.

**Responsibilities**

* Install new HVAC systems, including air conditioning units, ventilation systems, boilers, and refrigeration equipment, following schematics and HVAC design plans.
* Perform routine maintenance and troubleshooting on existing HVAC equipment to optimize performance and extend lifespan.
* Read and interpret technical schematics, blueprints, and mechanical drawings to accurately install and repair systems.
* Conduct service calls for appliance repair, property maintenance issues, or system failures in various settings.
* Use diagnostic tools such as ohmmeters to identify electrical issues; perform welding or mechanical repairs as needed.
* Maintain detailed records of work performed, including service reports, installation logs, and maintenance schedules.
* Ensure compliance with safety standards and local building codes during all installation and repair activities.

**Requirements**

* Proven experience as a service technician in HVAC or related fields such as refrigeration, plumbing, or mechanical maintenance.
* Strong knowledge of HVAC systems, refrigeration cycles (including ammonia refrigeration), and HVAC/R (Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning/ Refrigeration) principles.
* Ability to read schematics and mechanical drawings accurately; familiarity with HVAC design is a plus.
* Mechanical knowledge that includes boiler operation, appliance repair, welding skills, and property maintenance.
* Field service experience with the ability to troubleshoot electrical components using tools like ohmmeters.
* Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record; \\<PERSON\\> driver certification preferred.
* Additional skills such as welding, equipment repair, boiler operation, or mechanic experience are highly desirable.
* Physical ability to perform manual tasks including lifting heavy equipment and working in confined spaces. Join us in delivering top\\-tier HVAC solutions! We are committed to fostering a vibrant work environment where your skills grow through hands\\-on experience in construction projects and system installations. This paid position offers the chance to advance your career while making a tangible impact on our clients’ comfort and safety.

Pay: From $1,000\\.00 per day

Work Location: In person

title: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Technician
description:
Job Summary

Rocky HVAC Services is seeking a skilled and dependable HVAC Service Technician to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and repairing heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems for residential and light commercial clients. This role requires strong technical expertise, problem\\-solving ability, and a commitment to delivering high\\-quality customer service.

Key Responsibilities

* Diagnose, troubleshoot, and repair HVAC systems including air conditioners, furnaces, heat pumps, and ventilation units
* Perform routine maintenance and inspections to ensure optimal system performance
* Install new HVAC systems and components in accordance with company standards and local codes
* Communicate effectively with customers regarding system issues, repair options, and maintenance recommendations
* Maintain accurate service records, invoices, and documentation
* Ensure compliance with safety standards and company policies
* Respond to emergency service calls as needed

Qualifications

* Proven experience as an HVAC Technician or similar role
* EPA Certification (required)
* Strong knowledge of HVAC systems, electrical components, and refrigerant handling
* Ability to read blueprints and technical diagrams
* Valid driver’s license and clean driving record
* Strong troubleshooting and critical thinking skills
* Excellent customer service and communication abilities

Preferred Qualifications

* NATE Certification (a plus)
* Experience with smart thermostats and modern HVAC controls
* Prior experience in residential and light commercial service

Work Environment \\& Physical Requirements

* Ability to lift up to 50 lbs and work in confined or elevated spaces
* Comfortable working in varying weather conditions
* Frequent standing, bending, and climbing

Compensation \\& Benefits

* Competitive hourly pay based on experience
* Overtime opportunities
* Paid holidays
* Company vehicle (if applicable)
* Ongoing training and career development

Why Join Rocky HVAC Services?

At Rocky HVAC Services, we value professionalism, reliability, and craftsmanship. We provide a supportive work environment where technicians can grow their skills and build long\\-term careers.

Apply today to become part of a trusted and growing HVAC team!

Job Type: Full\\-time

Pay: $25\\.00 \\- $40\\.00 per hour

MMS Group is seeking an experienced **B****uilding** **Maintenance Technician** for a **108\\-unit** residential property located in **Columbia, MD**.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Regular Maintenance: Maintain residential apartment homes and other areas on the property, including building exteriors, interiors, and public areas, regularly.
* Repairs: Perform small appliance, plumbing, electrical, and other repairs in residential apartments and other areas of the property.
* Installation: Install tile and laminate flooring

**Desired Skills:**

Are you the person we’re looking for? Apply now. Visit us at www.mmsgroup.com for more details!

description:

**Sign\\-On Bonus:** Up to $15,000\\.00 for Journeyman Licensed Techs (based on experience and interview)  

**Schedule:** 4\\-Day Work Week \\| 5\\-Day Work Week \\| No On\\-Call  

**Full\\-time, year\\-round work**  

Busy residential HVAC team near Pasadena is looking for Journeyman Licensed Service Technicians to take on a strong pipeline of service calls.  

Blue Dot Services of Maryland is building a stronger HVAC team focused on support, professionalism, and doing the job right. If you’re a Licensed HVAC Technician who takes pride in your work, APPLY NOW.  

  

What we offer:* **Competitive hourly pay: $25\\.00/hr up to $40\\.00/hr**
* **4 and 5 day work weeks available, no on\\-call**
* Insurance available after 31 days (medical, dental, vision, HSA / FSA)
* Low\\-cost medical (as low as $5/week)
* 401(k) with company match
* 13 days PTO \\+ 8 paid holidays
* Company\\-paid life insurance
* Take\\-home vehicle \\+ gas card
* Uniforms with cleaning service
* Weekly direct deposit
* Experienced technicians will be provided with training towards Journeyman and Master License as well as other certifications.

  

As a **Residential HVAC Service Technician**, you will diagnose, service, and repair home comfort systems across Maryland. This role is hands\\-on and customer\\-facing — you’ll work inside homes (including attics and crawl spaces) to troubleshoot issues, complete quality repairs, and restore systems safely and professionally. You’ll be trusted to work independently, represent the company well, and deliver the kind of workmanship customers remember.**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Diagnose and repair residential HVAC systems with speed, accuracy, and professionalism
* Perform service and repairs on:
	+ **Split systems**
	+ **Heat pumps**
	+ **Gas furnaces**
	+ **Boilers** (gas/oil where applicable)
	+ **Air handlers**
	+ **Thermostats / zoning controls**
	+ **Indoor air quality components** (humidifiers, UV lights, filtration, etc.)
* Troubleshoot electrical, airflow, refrigerant, ignition, venting, and control issues
* Confirm system performance after repair (temps, pressures, amps, static pressure when needed)
* Communicate clearly with homeowners: problem, solution, pricing, and expectations
* Document diagnostics, recommended repairs, and completed work in the system (notes, photos, invoices)
* Work safely in residential environments including **tight spaces, ladders, attics, crawl spaces as needed**
* Maintain a clean and professional service vehicle, tools, and appearance
* Follow all company safety procedures, EPA guidelines, and Maryland licensing requirements

**What You Need**
-----------------

* **Maryland Journeyman HVAC License required**
* **EPA Certification Required**
* Valid driver’s license and clean driving record
* Able to pass background and drug screening
* Ability to work in attics, crawlspaces, and lift heavy equipment

Additional compensation may be offered based on experience and will be outlined in an offer letter addendum. title: Residential Service Plumber
description:
We are seeking a skilled and dedicated Residential Service Technician Plumber to join our team. In this role, you will provide top\\-notch plumbing services to residential clients, ensuring their plumbing systems operate efficiently and reliably. As a key member of our service team, you’ll diagnose, repair, and maintain a wide range of plumbing systems while delivering exceptional customer service.

Key Responsibilities:

* Diagnose and repair plumbing issues, including leaks, clogs, water heaters, faucets, toilets, and pipe systems.
* Perform routine maintenance and inspections of residential plumbing systems.
* Install, replace, or upgrade plumbing fixtures and systems as needed.
* Respond promptly to service calls, troubleshoot problems, and provide effective solutions.
* Educate customers on plumbing system care and recommend appropriate services or products.
* Maintain accurate records of work performed, including service reports and billing information.
* Adhere to safety protocols, local plumbing codes, and company standards.
* Collaborate with team members and communicate effectively with clients to ensure satisfaction.

Qualifications:

* High school diploma or equivalent; completion of a plumbing apprenticeship or trade school preferred.
* 2\\+ years of experience as a residential plumber or service technician.
* Strong knowledge of plumbing systems, tools, and diagnostic techniques.
* Excellent problem\\-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a fast\\-paced environment.
* Outstanding customer service and communication skills.
* Valid driver’s license and clean driving record.
* Ability to lift heavy equipment and work in confined spaces or at heights.

Preferred Skills:

* Experience with modern plumbing technologies, such as tankless water heaters or smart home plumbing systems.
* Familiarity with sewer line repairs, drain cleaning, or hydro\\-jetting.

Benefits:

* Competitive salary and performance\\-based bonuses.
* Health, dental, and vision insurance.
* Paid time off and holidays.
* Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
* Company\\-provided tools, uniform, and service vehicle.
* Retirement plan with company match.

Why Join Us? Be part of a reputable, customer\\-focused plumbing company that values quality, integrity, and teamwork. We offer a supportive work environment, opportunities for growth, and the chance to make a positive impact in the communities we serve.

How to Apply: If you’re a motivated plumber with a passion for solving problems and delivering excellent service, we’d love to hear from you!

**Title:**


Facilities Engineering Technician**Belong. Connect. Grow. with KBR!**


We at KBR share one goal: to improve the world responsibly as a company of innovators, thinkers, creators, explorers, volunteers, and dreamers**.**

**Position Summary**


The Facilities Engineering Technician supports the operation, maintenance, and improvement of building systems and infrastructure. This role performs hands\\-on technical work to ensure facilities, equipment, and utilities operate safely, efficiently, and in compliance with applicable codes, standards, and organizational requirements.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Perform preventive and corrective maintenance on building systems, including:
	+ HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and mechanical systems
	+ Building automation and monitoring systems
	+ Life safety systems (fire alarm, fire suppression)
* Troubleshoot, diagnose, and repair facility equipment and infrastructure issues.
* Support facility upgrades, renovations, and engineering projects, including installation and testing of new equipment.
* Conduct facility inspections and walkthroughs to identify maintenance needs and safety issues.
* Read and interpret technical drawings, manuals, schematics, and preventive maintenance procedures.
* Document work performed, corrective actions, and equipment status in maintenance tracking systems.
* Coordinate with contractors and vendors for specialized repairs and services.
* Support regulatory compliance and audits by maintaining records and assisting with inspections.
* Follow all safety, environmental, and security procedures.
* Provide after\\-hours or on\\-call support as required.

**Required Qualifications**

* High school diploma or GED required.
* Associate degree or technical certification in Engineering Technology, Industrial Maintenance, HVAC, Electrical, Mechanical, or related field preferred.
* Minimum **2–5 years** of experience in facilities maintenance, engineering support, or similar technical role.
* Working knowledge of building systems (HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and mechanical).
* Ability to troubleshoot equipment and systems using standard diagnostic tools.
* Basic computer skills and experience using maintenance management systems (CMMS).
* Ability to lift up to 50 lbs and perform physical tasks associated with maintenance work.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Certifications such as EPA Universal, HVAC, Electrical, or Facilities Maintenance certification.
* Experience in regulated environments (government, laboratory, healthcare, manufacturing).
* Familiarity with building codes, OSHA regulations, and safety standards.
* Experience coordinating work with engineers, inspectors, or external contractors.

**Basic Compensation:**

We support career advancement through professional training and development.

title: Maintenance Technician
description:
**Job Title**
=============


Maintenance Technician**Job Description Summary**
===========================


Responsible to assist with the operation troubleshooting, diagnostics, housekeeping, and repairs on commercial buildings, industrial systems, vehicles, grounds, and maintenance equipment for assigned properties/facilities.**Job Description**
===================

*Job Description*

* Assist with troubleshooting and repairs of buildings and installed systems to include: packaging and handling machinery, plumbing systems, kitchen equipment, roofs, drains, shop, grounds equipment, and HVAC
* Clean, lubricate, and service a wide variety of pumps, valves, pneumatic controls, electrical, mechanical, and hydraulic devices
* Assist with installation and modification of building equipment and systems
* Replace and repair building finishes such as ceiling tiles, door hardware, wall paper, and can paint walls, piping, floors and equipment
* Troubleshoot, evaluate, and make recommendations to upgrade maintenance operations and/or implement savings opportunities
* Possess competent knowledge of the use and care of tools and equipment used in facility and ground maintenance
* Respond immediately to emergency situations and customer service requests as assigned.
* Inspect buildings, grounds, and equipment for unsafe or malfunctioning conditions, preventative maintenance, etc.
* Maintain and operate fire and life safety systems such as; Fire alarm systems, fire pumps and related fire protection system equipment as assigned
* Perform carpentry and snow removal when necessary
* Comply with all applicable codes, regulations, governmental agency, and Company directives as related to building operations and practice safe work habits
* Ability to use technology such as; smart phones, tablets, computers, web\\-based applications, building automation systems, etc.
* Complete all required C\\&W Safety Training as scheduled annually.
* Comply with C\\&W Uniform Dress Code while working and maintain a neat and clean appearance while on the property at times other than working hours

  

KEY COMPETENCIES  

* Technical Proficiency
* Initiative
* Flexibility
* Multi\\-Tasking
* Sense of Urgency
* High School Diploma or GED equivalent


IMPORTANT EXPERIENCE  

* 2\\+ years of related experience in a commercial property setting

  

ADDITIONAL ELIGIBILITY QUALIFICATIONS  

* Technical, vocational or on\\-the\\-job training in at least one of the following areas: HVAC, electrical, mechanical, process controls, mechanical power transmissions, painting, plumbing, carpentry or engine repair
* Possess and maintain a valid driver’s license (commercial license may be required) and good driving record with periodic checks
* Basic Computing Skills in Outlook, Excel \\& Word


May be only maintenance staff member on duty during certain shifts; may be required to work extended periods of time without relief when responding to priority/emergency situations (including overtime type assignments); may require shift work and/or stand\\-by on\\-call duties
WORK ENVIRONMENT  

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines.


PHYSICAL DEMANDS  

The PHYSICAL DEMANDS described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Must have ability to stoop ,stand ,climb ,frequently lift a minimum of 50lbs.of equipment ( pumps ,tools ,ladders)and safety install rigging/lifting devices.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to communicate with others and exchange accurate information; operate a computer and other office productivity machinery; move about the workplace; remain in a stationary position for 10% of the time; and extend hands and arms in any direction.

description:
**\\<Job Summary\\>**  
Join our dynamic team as a Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Technician, where you'll play a vital role in maintaining and repairing essential climate control systems across commercial and residential properties. Your expertise will ensure optimal indoor comfort, energy efficiency, and safety. This position offers an exciting opportunity to apply your mechanical skills, technical knowledge, and field service experience in a fast\\-paced environment that values proactive problem\\-solving and customer satisfaction.

**\\<Duties\\>**

* Install, troubleshoot, repair, and maintain HVAC systems including air conditioning units, boilers, refrigeration equipment, and ventilation systems.
* Read and interpret schematics, blueprints, and technical manuals to accurately diagnose issues and implement effective solutions.
* Conduct routine inspections of HVAC equipment to identify potential problems before they escalate, ensuring continuous operation.
* Perform plumbing tasks related to HVAC systems such as pipe fitting, drainage, and refrigerant line connections.
* Utilize specialized tools such as ohmmeters for electrical testing and welding equipment for repairs requiring metal fabrication.
* Assist with property maintenance tasks including appliance repair, mechanical system upgrades, and emergency repairs as needed.

**\\<Requirements\\>**

* Proven experience as an HVAC service technician or in a related mechanical field with a strong understanding of HVAC/R (Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning / Refrigeration) systems.
* Knowledge of construction practices relevant to installing or upgrading HVAC systems within various building types.
* Ability to read schematics, technical drawings, and HVAC design plans accurately.
* Familiarity with ammonia refrigeration systems and the use of diagnostic tools such as ohmmeters.
* Mechanical knowledge encompassing boiler operation, equipment repair, welding techniques, and general property maintenance.
* Experience working in field service environments with a focus on appliance repair and property upkeep.
* Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record; experience operating \\<PERSON\\> drivers preferred for transporting equipment or parts.
* Certifications or training in HVAC/R technology; additional qualifications in plumbing or refrigeration are advantageous.
* Strong problem\\-solving skills coupled with excellent communication abilities to effectively interact with clients and team members. Embark on a career where your technical expertise makes a tangible difference! We’re committed to supporting your growth through ongoing training opportunities while providing a collaborative environment that celebrates innovation and dedication. Join us to help keep buildings comfortable, safe, and energy\\-efficient every day!

Pay: $30\\.00 per hour

Expected hours: 20\\.0 – 40\\.0 per week

Description:
**WHO WE ARE**


Founded in 1969, Summit Point Motorsports Park and Training Facility provides Commercial Motorsports events along with customized Security and Emergency Medical training solutions on our 786\\-acre facility located in Summit Point, WV. We are a recognized leader in the Motorsports and Security Training Solutions Markets for both commercial and government clients. We take pride in providing tailored solutions for clients in an ever\\-evolving world.

**WHAT YOU'LL DO**


Facility Maintenance Technicians are responsible for supporting the maintenance, repair, and improvement of buildings, infrastructure, equipment, and grounds across a dynamic operational environment. Technicians in this role will work across a variety of trades and environments, including building systems, grounds, equipment, and motorsports\\-related infrastructure. Work ranges from routine maintenance and repairs to complex troubleshooting and field coordination.

**The ideal candidate will be responsible for:**

* Perform maintenance, repair, and troubleshooting of buildings, systems, equipment, and site infrastructure
* Execute preventative maintenance tasks and document completed work
* Respond to maintenance issues, emergency repairs, and operational needs
* Operate tools and equipment appropriate to assigned tasks and certifications
* Support facility readiness for events, inspections, and operational demands
* Identify safety concerns, maintenance risks, and repair priorities
* Maintain accurate work documentation, material usage, and follow\\-up needs
* Assist in maintaining clean, safe, and operationally sound facilities and grounds

**Depending on experience level, work may involve:**

* Electrical systems
* HVAC systems
* Plumbing
* Carpentry and general construction
* Welding / fabrication
* Grounds, drainage, fencing, and track infrastructure
* Equipment and vehicle maintenance
* Heavy equipment operation (skid steers, tractors, forklifts, trailers)
* Motorsports facility systems and support infrastructure

**Work Environment:**

* On\\-site position.
* This role is non\\-exempt.

**WHY JOIN US**

* Be a part of a dynamic team at a premier training and motorsports facility.
* Opportunities for professional growth and development.
* Competitive compensation and benefits package.


Requirements:
**Job Requirements and Qualifications:**

**Required:**

* Minimum of 2 years of experience in facilities maintenance or a related field.
* Demonstrates a strong understanding of the general tasks and responsibilities associated with facilities maintenance, including building systems, equipment, and safety protocols.
* Capable of working independently while following established procedures and maintenance schedules to ensure the proper functioning of facilities.
* Proficient in multiple key areas, such as HVAC systems, plumbing, electrical, and general repairs, relevant to the role.
* Must be able to pass a background check and drug screening.
* Must have a high school diploma or equivalent.
* Must have a valid driver's license.

**Preferred:**

* Additional specialized training and/or certification related to facilities maintenance.

**PHYSICAL REQUIRMENTS/WORKING CONDITIONS**


This position is very active and requires climbing, balancing, stooping, kneeling, crouching, crawling, reaching, standing, walking, pushing, pulling, lifting, use of hands, grasping, feeling, talking, hearing, and repetitive motions all day. The employee must frequently lift and move items up to 100 pounds.


Typical assignments are frequently performed in both internal and external environments with a variety of weather conditions. Ability to work under extreme hot or cold weather conditions with exposure to dust or dirt and hazards is routine and may require the use of protective personal equipment. Most of the work is performed in outside weather conditions. The worker may be subject to atmospheric conditions, is frequently in close quarters, and is required to function in narrow aisles or passageways.

Founded in 1935, Roto\\-Rooter is North America's largest plumbing, drain cleaning, and water cleanup services provider. Roto\\-Rooter operates businesses in over 100 company\\-owned branches, independent contractor territories, and approximately 400 independent franchise operations, serving approximately 90% of the U.S. population and parts of Canada.  

If job security, top pay, excellent benefits, and career advancement representing a well\\-known national brand are what you're looking for, Roto\\-Rooter is the company for you. We pride ourselves on being a great place to work, providing excellent pay (our most experienced plumbers earn more than $100,000 per year), flexible work schedules and top benefits.  

Our plumbers provide a wide range of plumbing services, including faucet repairs, water heater installations, and plumbing system replacements.  

Salary Range for this position: **$110,000\\-$130,000** per year. Commissioned, non\\-exempt position


Responsibilities:
* Responds promptly to customer service calls, conducts thorough inspections of plumbing issues, accurately diagnoses problems, offers effective solutions and estimates, and successfully closes service sales.
* With the necessary licensing, proficiently installs and repairs various plumbing fixtures including sinks, toilets, bathtubs, water heaters, garbage disposal units, dishwashers, and water softeners.
* Conduct sewer and drain cleaning services in diverse environments such as basements, roofs, confined spaces, and close quarters. Ensure thorough cleaning and restoration of work sites to meet pre\\-job conditions.
* Prepares invoice, totaling costs of services/products provided. Collects payment and explains Roto\\-Rooter guarantee, both in writing and on provided smartphone.

Requirements:
* A valid driver’s license.

* Full knowledge of plumbing systems is essential for performing duties.

Journeyman license or equivalent certification as specified by the state in which branch operates and performs service. Unlicensed plumbers will be expected to obtain certification as soon as they become eligible, if necessary, by state law.
* Must have good written and oral communication skills for effective customer service. Must have excellent communication and interpersonal skills in client and co\\-worker relations.

**Appliance Service Technician** *Monday \\- Friday 6:00 am \\- 4:00 pm*  

Are you a problem\\-solver who loves to be out in the field? Our Appliance Techs provide mechanical repairs to washers and dryers at customer and client locations within their assigned region. Appliance Technicians keep our machines running smoothly to meet customer expectations or the need for repeated service needs. The ideal candidate is someone who enjoys working independently and has a very strong mechanical aptitude. **Key Responsibilities:**

* **Service and Repair**: Perform diagnosis, troubleshooting, and repair of appliances such as washing machines, dryers, and air\\-vending machines. Ensure that all repairs are carried out efficiently and in accordance with manufacturer guidelines and safety standards.

* **Documentation**: Maintain detailed records of service calls, repairs performed, parts used, and any other relevant information. Prepare service reports and update the internal database with accurate and up\\-to\\-date information.
* **Parts and Inventory Management**: Monitor and manage inventory levels of spare parts and tools. Order necessary parts and equipment to ensure the availability of required resources for efficient repairs and maintenance.
* **Safety Compliance**: Adhere to all safety protocols and guidelines while working with electrical systems and appliances. Ensure personal safety and the safety of customers' property during service calls.

* Valid Driver's License

* Physical stamina and dexterity to handle and maneuver heavy appliances.

Are you ready to **be Part of Something BIGGER!**

**About this role:**


An HVAC Service Technician is responsible for the service, maintenance, and repair of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems in residential and commercial settings. This role involves diagnosing system issues, performing routine and emergency repairs, and ensuring systems operate efficiently and safely. Technicians interact with customers to explain problems and solutions, adhere to safety regulations, and maintain a clean and organized work environment. They may also be involved in system inspections, adjustments, and upgrades, while keeping detailed records of service performed. Strong technical skills, customer service, and adherence to industry standards are crucial for success in this role.

**In this role, you will be responsible for:**

* Clean, adjust, and repair HVAC systems, including air handling units, ductwork, vents, and condensation drainage.
* Diagnose and perform service work on HVAC systems in residential settings to ensure optimal performance.
* Provide expert HVAC solutions to customers, addressing their needs and offering professional recommendations.

**Required Qualifications:**

* 3\\-5\\+ years of HVAC experience, preferably with a focus on residential markets

* Possesses strong troubleshooting and critical thinking skills for resolving complex HVAC issues.
* Demonstrates a positive attitude and a strong commitment to high\\-quality workmanship.
* Prioritizes exceptional customer service and effectively addresses diverse client needs.
* Skilled in interacting professionally with individuals at all organizational levels.
* Flexible and innovative team player, thriving in dynamic environments and adapting to new challenges.

**The physical requirements of this job include:**

  

* Lifting 50 pounds regularly and up to 100 pounds occasionally
* Climbing ladders, accessing attics, navigating tight spaces, driving, entering crawlspaces, pushing, carrying, pulling, using hand tools, and performing fine detail work.
* Ability to drive.
* Working in various environments, including indoor and outdoor settings, attics, crawlspaces.
* Ability to stand, walk and sit; talk or hear, both in person and by telephone.
* Use hands to handle or feel objects or controls; reach with hands and arms.
* Regularly stoop, kneel, bend, and crouch.

description:
**Compressed Air Systems Diagnostic Technician**
================================================


**Service region:** Fredericksburg • Stafford • Williamsburg • Petersburg, VA


**Make reliability your mission**
---------------------------------


Picture a plant where compressed air is the heartbeat. A line goes down. You arrive, evaluate a rotary screw, reciprocating, Centac, or ECC compressor with precision gauges, and restore operations. You leave behind a stable system, clear documentation, and a customer who trusts your expertise. That is the impact you will own every day at Ingersoll Rand.

**Commission and care:** Install and commission Ingersoll Rand equipment; maintain and repair compressors and supporting components like dryers, filters, and cooling systems. **Diagnose with confidence:** Apply precision measuring tools and OEM operations/parts manuals to troubleshoot across multi\\-brand systems. **Educate and advise:** Train customers and field colleagues; translate diagnostics into actionable steps. **Operate independently:** Assess root causes, recommend remedies, and submit accurate reports and verbal updates to the factory without close supervision. **Model professionalism:** Uphold integrity, keep communications crisp, and ensure reports and expenses are thorough, legible, and on time. Steward company assets (credit cards, vehicle if assigned) responsibly.
**What you bring**
------------------


* 2–5 years troubleshooting industrial/commercial mechanical systems; familiarity with compressed air, centrifugal technologies, and control systems is a plus. Experience launching and servicing rotary and centrifugal compressors, dryers, cooling towers, and filtration is desirable.
* High school diploma or equivalent.
* Driver’s license held for 12\\+ months with no major or frequent violations (no DUI in the last 5 years; no hit \\& run, suspensions, reckless/careless driving; and no pattern of minor infractions or preventable collisions in the prior 3 years).

**Skills that set you up to win**

* Microsoft Office proficiency and solid computer literacy.
* Comfort with handheld/mobile computing (smartphone, iPad) and networked tools.
* Customer\\-first mindset: communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution.
* Smart travel habits that balance responsiveness and cost.
* Mechanical aptitude to master compressor systems and machinery.

**Preferred credentials**
-------------------------


* Electrical/Electronic Technology or HVAC studies covering AC/DC circuits, wiring diagrams/schematics, motors/controls, PLCs, HVAC electricity and controls, refrigeration systems, and/or refrigerant certification. Military background or equivalent mechanical experience is a plus; equivalent knowledge without a formal program is considered.
* EPA 608 / F\\-GAS 303/2008/CE/ISHRAE certification preferred.

**Where and how you will work**
-------------------------------


* Exposure to mechanical rooms and industrial sites with loud equipment, inclement weather, heights, and high\\-voltage systems per NFPA or equivalent standards.

**Travel and on\\-call**
-----------------------


* Training travel 1–2 times per year, about one week each (5–10%).

**Grow with us**
----------------


We practice a culture of personal ownership. Our benefits help you take charge of your well\\-being and future: medical and prescription coverage, dental and vision plans, wellness programs, life insurance, a strong 401(k), paid time off, and an employee stock grant—plus additional offerings.


**About Ingersoll Rand**

Our teams perform in the harshest conditions and create customers for life through expertise, productivity, and efficiency. Discover more at www.IRCO.com.

Ingersoll Rand is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

* Motor Replacement/Repair/Maintenance

description:
**Job Summary**  
Join our dynamic team as a Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Technician, where you will be at the forefront of maintaining and repairing essential climate control systems across diverse properties. Your expertise will ensure optimal indoor air quality, energy efficiency, and system reliability. This role offers an exciting opportunity for motivated technicians to apply their mechanical knowledge, troubleshoot complex HVAC/R systems, and deliver outstanding service in a fast\\-paced environment. Whether working on new installations or performing routine maintenance, your skills will directly impact the comfort and safety of our clients’ spaces.

**Duties**

* Install, inspect, troubleshoot, repair, and maintain HVAC systems including air conditioning units, boilers, refrigeration equipment, and ventilation systems.
* Read and interpret schematics, technical manuals, and HVAC design plans to accurately diagnose issues and execute repairs.
* Perform plumbing work related to HVAC systems such as condensate drain lines and water supply connections.
* Conduct field service visits to client sites for routine maintenance or urgent repairs on HVAC/R equipment.
* Utilize tools such as ohmmeters and welding equipment to test electrical components and perform mechanical repairs.
* Diagnose system malfunctions using mechanical knowledge and technical expertise; recommend solutions to optimize performance.
* Maintain detailed service records, complete inspection reports, and document all repairs performed for accountability and future reference.
* Operate a \\<PERSON\\> driver vehicle safely while transporting tools, parts, or equipment to job sites.
* Assist with HVAC system design considerations for new installations or upgrades based on client needs and building specifications.
* Support property maintenance tasks related to heating and cooling systems to ensure continuous operation and compliance with safety standards.

**Qualifications**

* Proven experience as a service technician specializing in HVAC/R systems with a strong background in construction or property maintenance.
* Knowledge of schematics, HVAC design principles, refrigeration cycles, ammonia refrigeration systems, and plumbing fundamentals.
* Mechanical aptitude with experience in equipment repair including boilers, appliances, welding techniques, and general maintenance tasks.
* Familiarity with electrical testing tools such as ohmmeters; ability to read electrical schematics accurately.
* Hands\\-on experience working with refrigeration units—particularly ammonia refrigeration—is highly desirable.
* Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record; ability to operate a \\<PERSON\\> driver vehicle safely across various sites.
* Strong mechanical knowledge complemented by experience in field service environments involving appliance repair or HVAC system troubleshooting.
* Excellent problem\\-solving skills combined with the ability to work independently or as part of a team in diverse settings. Join us if you’re passionate about delivering reliable climate control solutions! We’re committed to supporting your growth through ongoing training opportunities in HVAC/R technology and maintenance practices—empowering you to excel in this energetic role!

* Tools provided
* Vision insurance

**Additional Information**
